findings.title: Dashboard Load Time Optimization
findings.description: Optimize the dashboard load time which has regressed from 1.2s to 4.8s after the v3.2 deployment, affecting user experience.
assumptions: The regression is primarily due to unoptimized SVG rendering in the new charting library.

evidence.excerpt: Dashboard load time regressed from 1.2s to 4.8s after v3.2 deploy.

recommendations: Investigate and optimize SVG rendering in the charting library.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the dashboard is loaded WHEN the user accesses the dashboard THEN the load time should be <= 2s.

findings.title: API Latency Improvement
findings.description: Address the API response time degradation from 120ms to 380ms after the database migration.
assumptions: The degradation is due to a query planner regression in PostgreSQL.

evidence.excerpt: API response times degraded 3x after database migration.

recommendations: Analyze and optimize the query planner for time-series aggregation.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the API is called WHEN a request is made THEN the response time should be <= 200ms.

findings.title: WebSocket Memory Leak Fix
findings.description: Resolve the memory leak issue in WebSocket connections that leads to server crashes.
assumptions: The memory leak is caused by event listener accumulation.

evidence.excerpt: Memory leak in real-time WebSocket connections.

recommendations: Implement proper cleanup of event listeners in the WebSocket connection.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a WebSocket connection is active WHEN it runs for 8 hours THEN memory usage should not exceed 100MB.

findings.title: Automated Performance Regression Detection
findings.description: Implement automated performance regression detection in the CI pipeline to catch regressions before deployment.
assumptions: Automated benchmarks can effectively catch performance regressions.

evidence.excerpt: Implement performance regression detection in CI pipeline.

recommendations: Create a benchmark suite that runs on every PR.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a PR is created WHEN benchmarks are run THEN no regression should exceed 15% in p95 latency.

findings.title: Customer Status Page Development
findings.description: Build a public status page that shows real-time performance metrics to improve customer visibility during incidents.
assumptions: Customers need visibility into performance metrics during incidents.

evidence.excerpt: Customer-facing status page showing real-time performance metrics.

recommendations: Design and implement a status page with historical data for the last 90 days.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the status page is accessed WHEN an incident occurs THEN real-time metrics should be displayed.

findings.title: Performance Monitoring Metrics Analysis
findings.description: Analyze customer support tickets related to performance issues to identify root causes.
assumptions: Analyzing support tickets will reveal trends in performance issues.

evidence.excerpt: There is a gap in the analysis of customer support tickets.

recommendations: Conduct a thorough analysis of performance-related support tickets.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN support tickets are analyzed WHEN trends are identified THEN a report should be generated.

findings.title: Performance Monitoring Dashboard Enhancements
findings.description: Enhance the performance monitoring dashboard to include additional metrics and visualizations.
assumptions: Enhanced visualizations will improve user understanding of performance metrics.

evidence.excerpt: The North Star metric for PageSpeed is the p95 load time of the dashboard.

recommendations: Add visualizations for p50, p95, and p99 latencies.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the dashboard is accessed WHEN performance metrics are displayed THEN p50, p95, and p99 latencies should be visible.

findings.title: Error Rate Monitoring Implementation
findings.description: Implement monitoring for error rate percentage as a guardrail metric.
assumptions: Monitoring error rates will help maintain user satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: The error rate percentage is a guardrail metric that must not degrade.

recommendations: Set up alerts for error rate thresholds.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN error rate monitoring is implemented WHEN the error rate exceeds 5% THEN alerts should be triggered.

findings.title: User Feedback Collection Mechanism
findings.description: Implement a mechanism for collecting user feedback on performance issues.
assumptions: User feedback will provide insights into performance issues.

evidence.excerpt: Customers lack visibility into performance metrics during incidents.

recommendations: Create a feedback form accessible from the dashboard.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback form is accessed WHEN a user submits feedback THEN the feedback should be recorded.

findings.title: Performance Monitoring Alerts Setup
findings.description: Set up alerts for performance metrics to notify the team of regressions.
assumptions: Alerts will help the team respond quickly to performance issues.
recommendations: Configure alerts for p95 latency and error rates.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN performance metrics are monitored WHEN thresholds are exceeded THEN alerts should be sent to the team.

findings.title: Performance Benchmarking Documentation
findings.description: Document the performance benchmarking process and results for transparency and future reference.
assumptions: Documentation will help maintain performance standards.

evidence.excerpt: Implement performance regression detection in CI pipeline.

recommendations: Create a comprehensive document detailing the benchmarking process.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the documentation is created WHEN it is reviewed THEN it should provide clear guidelines on benchmarking.

summary: The analysis identified critical performance regressions affecting the PageSpeed platform, leading to the creation of a structured backlog of requirements focused on optimizing dashboard load times, API latency, WebSocket stability, and enhancing customer visibility during incidents. Each requirement is prioritized and includes specific acceptance criteria to ensure measurable outcomes.
